user_message: I have this escalation email from Contoso Ltd that needs identify the blockers. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-01-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into forecast prep.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:08:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:20:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:24:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-N -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
